auth_notes: Visual FoxPro (VFP) is a legacy file-based database and does not have a native web API. Authentication must be implemented via a custom-built REST wrapper (e.g., using Python, .NET, or Node.js) or through a database bridge. The auth type will be whatever is defined by that middleware (typically API Key or Basic Auth).
rate_limits: N/A (Limited by file system I/O and middleware implementation)
gotchas: File Locking: VFP uses table-level locking; concurrent writes from an API can cause 'File is in use' errors or data corruption.
Legacy Drivers: Requires 32-bit OLE DB providers which are difficult to run on modern 64-bit Linux environments where n8n often resides.
Data Types: Modern JSON types may not map cleanly to legacy FoxPro field types (e.g., Logical, General, or Memo fields).
client_must_provide: A Windows-based host to run the OLE DB/ODBC driver
Custom middleware/API wrapper to expose the data
research_notes: Direct integration is impossible. The recommended approach is to build a small Python Flask or FastAPI service using the 'dbfread' or 'pyodbc' library to act as a bridge. n8n will then use the HTTP Request node to communicate with this bridge. If n8n is self-hosted on Windows, the 'Execute Command' node could theoretically run local scripts to manipulate the files.

effort_recommendation.rationale: Direct integration is impossible. The recommended approach is to build a small Python Flask or FastAPI service using the 'dbfread' or 'pyodbc' library to act as a bridge. n8n will then use the HTTP Request node to communicate with this bridge. If n8n is self-hosted on Windows, the 'Execute Command' node could theoretically run local scripts to manipulate the files.
